name: IEEE Conference Paper
description: Standard IEEE two-column conference paper. Strong fit for engineering, CS, and applied-AI submissions.

sections.title: Title, Authors & Affiliations
sections.purpose: Identify the paper, all authors, and their affiliations per IEEE front-matter conventions.
sections.formatting_notes: Single-line title in Title Case; authors centered below; affiliations on the next line.

sections.title: Abstract
sections.purpose: One-paragraph summary covering problem, method, results, and contribution.
sections.formatting_notes: Single paragraph, no citations, no acronyms before definition.

sections.title: Index Terms
sections.purpose: Provide IEEE index terms for discoverability.
sections.formatting_notes: Comma-separated list, lowercase except proper nouns.

sections.title: I. Introduction
sections.purpose: Establish problem importance, gap in prior work, and contributions of this paper.
sections.formatting_notes: Use Roman numeral section heading. End with a 'The remainder of this paper is organized as follows' paragraph.
sections.citation_example: [1] A. Author, "Title," IEEE Trans. X, vol. 12, no. 3, pp. 1-9, 2024.

sections.title: II. Related Work
sections.purpose: Position the work against the prior literature; identify the gap addressed.
what_to_include: Strengths and limitations of each thread
sections.formatting_notes: Group references by theme; do not list chronologically.
sections.citation_example: [2] B. Author and C. Author, "...," Proc. IEEE Conf. Y, pp. 10-18, 2023.

sections.title: III. Proposed Method
sections.purpose: Describe the proposed approach in enough detail that a competent reader can re-implement it.
sections.formatting_notes: Use subsections (A, B, C). Define every variable on first use. Reference figures and tables explicitly.

sections.title: IV. Experimental Setup
sections.purpose: Document datasets, baselines, hyperparameters, and evaluation metrics for reproducibility.
sections.formatting_notes: Use a comparison table for baselines; cite each dataset source.

sections.title: V. Results and Discussion
sections.purpose: Present experimental findings, statistical significance, and interpretive discussion.
what_to_include: Discussion of why the method works
sections.formatting_notes: Place results table near top of section; refer to it as 'Table I'. Use bold for best values.

sections.title: VI. Comparative Analysis
sections.purpose: Side-by-side comparison against state-of-the-art baselines on shared benchmarks.
sections.formatting_notes: Table II should list all baselines with same columns as Table I.

sections.title: VII. Conclusion
sections.purpose: Restate the contribution, summarize key results, and outline future work.
what_to_include: Two or three concrete future directions
sections.formatting_notes: Single subsection. No citations except when essential.

sections.title: Acknowledgements
sections.purpose: Credit funding bodies and non-author contributors.
sections.formatting_notes: Unnumbered section between conclusion and references.

sections.title: References
sections.purpose: List all cited works in IEEE numeric style, matching the inline markers used throughout.
sections.formatting_notes: Use IEEE bracket style: [1] J. Doe, "Title," Journal Name, vol. X, no. Y, pp. nn-nn, year.
sections.citation_example: [1] J. Doe, "A Title," IEEE Trans. X, vol. 12, no. 3, pp. 1-9, Mar. 2024.
